prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Run anomaly detection on transformer T-019 winding temperature for the past 7 days and summarize any persistent abnormal periods.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should identify abnormal intervals (if any), quantify severity or count of anomalies, and provide a concise interpretation of persistence.

## Agent Plan
(no plan)

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "turn": 1,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "",
    "tool_calls": [
      {
        "name": "detect_anomalies",
        "arguments": {
          "transformer_id": "T-019",
          "sensor_id": "winding_temp_c",
          "window_size": 168,
          "z_threshold": 3
        },
        "call_id": "chatcmpl-tool-8717b1fceec74893a6e72bfd6cfd21a3",
        "output": {
          "error": "No readings for transformer='T-019' sensor='winding_temp_c'."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "turn": 2,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "This function call will detect anomalies in the winding temperature sensor readings for transformer T-019 over the past 7 days (assuming a 24-hour window size). The z_threshold is set to 3, which means that any readings more than 3 standard deviations away from the mean will be considered anomalous. \n\nPlease note that the actual implementation of the detect_anomalies function is not provided, so this is just an example of how it could be used. \n\nAlso, the output of the iPython tool is an error message indicating that there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. This could be due to a variety of reasons such as the transformer or sensor not existing, or there being no readings for the past 7 days. \n\nIn a real-world scenario, you would need to handle this error and possibly provide a more informative error message or take alternative actions.",
    "tool_calls": []
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
This function call will detect anomalies in the winding temperature sensor readings for transformer T-019 over the past 7 days (assuming a 24-hour window size). The z_threshold is set to 3, which means that any readings more than 3 standard deviations away from the mean will be considered anomalous. 

Please note that the actual implementation of the detect_anomalies function is not provided, so this is just an example of how it could be used. 

Also, the output of the iPython tool is an error message indicating that there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. This could be due to a variety of reasons such as the transformer or sensor not existing, or there being no readings for the past 7 days. 

In a real-world scenario, you would need to handle this error and possibly provide a more informative error message or take alternative actions.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": false, "data_retrieval_accuracy": false, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": false, "clarity_and_justification": true, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "Handle missing data error and provide a more informative response."}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Handle missing data error and provide a more informative response.
